blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلتِّينِ وَٱلزَّيْتُونِ (۱)
By the fig and the olive, (1)
وَطُورِ سِينِينَ (۲)
by Mount Sinai, (2)
وَهَـٰذَا ٱلْبَلَدِ ٱلْأَمِينِ (۳)
and by this secure town: (3)
لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ فِىٓ أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ (٤)
We certainly created man in the best of forms; (4)
ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَـٰهُ أَسْفَلَ سَـٰفِلِينَ (٥)
then We relegated him to the lowest of the low, (5)
إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ (٦)
except those who have faith and do righteous deeds. There will be an everlasting reward for them. (6)
فَمَا يُكَذِّبُكَ بَعْدُ بِٱلدِّينِ (۷)
So what makes you deny the Retribution? (7)
أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِأَحْكَمِ ٱلْحَـٰكِمِينَ (۸)
Is not Allah the fairest of all judges? (8)

Key Points

Tur Sinin (Mount Sinai) is the same Sinai Valley between modern-day Egypt and Palestine. In some narrations, based on the context of "Tur Sinin" and "Al-Balad Al-Amin" (The Secure City), which refer to two sacred places, it is stated that "At-Tin wa Az-Zaytun" (The Fig and the Olive) also refers to the place where these two fruits grow, and the Ayah is understood to refer to the cities of Medina and Jerusalem (Bait al-Maqdis). (11) Among foodstuffs, fruits—and among them, sugary and fatty fruits—are most needed for the body's metabolism. "At-Tin wa Az-Zaytun" (The Fig and the Olive). One of the privileges of Islam is having a secure land, even for plants and animals. "Al-Balad Al-Amin" (The Secure City). The name fig (Tin) is mentioned once and olive (Zaytun) six times in the Quran, and both possess numerous nutritional and medicinal properties. Fig is beneficial for every age group due to the sugar it contains. Athletes and those suffering from weakness and old age are advised to eat it. Based on some narrations, the fig is a fruit of Paradise, and in traditional medicine, a concoction of fig and honey is recommended for stomach ulcers, and dried fig is recommended for strengthening the intellect. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Fig removes bad breath, strengthens the gums and bones, causes hair to grow, relieves pain, and is effective like medicine. Olive removes phlegm, brightens the complexion, strengthens the nerves, eliminates sickness, pain, and weakness, and extinguishes the fire of anger. (12) The meaning of "ثمّ رددناه اسفل سافلین" (Then We return him to the lowest of the low) is the abandonment by a group of humans of the primordial Divine nature (Fitrah) that Allah has placed within every human being. Whoever does this, Allah places him in the lowest of stations and he becomes afflicted by wretchedness and punishment.

11) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn. 12) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 66, p. 183, quoted from Tafsir-e Namuneh.

Messages

  1. Worldly blessings, even foodstuffs, possess sanctity and have been made the subject of a Divine oath. "و التین و الزیتون"
  2. Health achieved through food, and security, are the most important material needs of man. "التین و الزیتون... البلد الامین"
  3. The sanctity of revelation extends even to the lands. "طور سینین"
  4. The security of the city of Mecca was the prayer of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham), who said: "ربّ اجعل هذا بلداً آمنا" (My Lord, make this a secure city) (13), and this prayer was answered. "هذا البلد الامین"
  5. Man surpasses all creatures in creation. "خلقنا الانسان فى احسن تقویم"
  6. Allah governs the beginning and the end of man. "خلقنا... رددنا"
  7. Man has no baseness in his original creation; his fall occurs during the stages of life. "خلقنا الانسان فى احسن تقویم ثمّ رددناه اسفل سافلین"
  8. Man, who is the noblest creature, his fall is also lower than all other creatures. "احسن تقویم... اسفل سافلین" (Just as He says in other verses: "اولئك كالانعام بل هم اضلّ" (Those are like cattle; nay, they are even more astray) (14))
  9. Distance from faith and righteous deeds causes downfall, and faith is the factor that prevents all forms of baseness and decline. "رددناه اسفل سافلین الاّ الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات"
  10. Although Allah is the Creator of man and has the right to deal with His creation however He wills, yet He judges based on justice and is the Best of Judges. "احكم الحاكمین"

13) Al-Baqarah, 126. 14) Al-A'raf, 179.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah At-Tin
This Surah has eight verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse and means "The Fig." The central theme of the Surah is the reward and punishment of humans on the Day of Judgment, but it presents the matter through the creation of humans in the best form and their division into two groups: those who remain upon the pure Divine nature (Fitrah) and those who deviate from it and fall to the lowest degrees of humanity. This difference in choice leads to a difference in Divine reward and punishment. Naturally, wisdom dictates that the two groups should not be regarded as equal.
